loops.finding: Bluesky and Redsky audit packets were prose-first, so later workers could not reliably tell whether all required fields existed.
loops.implementation: Added a JSON gate with required Bluesky and Redsky sections for every loop.

bluesky.route_or_idea: Make audit loops parseable before they influence worker routing.
bluesky.best_case: Workers can reuse the same packet without losing route, measurement, stop, and decision fields.
bluesky.missing_measurement: A pass/fail count of complete loops.
bluesky.smallest_useful_experiment: Validate one five-loop fixture with all required fields present.
bluesky.bounded_alternative: Keep markdown docs, but require a JSON packet beside them.
bluesky.hidden_assumption: Workers will keep JSON concise enough to review.
bluesky.stop_condition: Stop if the packet needs private logs or route secrets.

redsky.route_or_claim: A prose-only audit can steer fleet work.
redsky.current_public_truth_checked: frontier d44cad3/q1152/1571592960; no validation or submit claim in this packet
redsky.strongest_objection: Prose can omit a kill gate while still sounding complete.
redsky.fastest_falsifier: Run the JSON gate and require all fields.

loops.finding: Evidence labels could drift between the optimistic pass and the adversarial pass.
loops.implementation: The gate checks allowed evidence labels and requires Redsky evidence to match the loop evidence.
loops.verification: mismatched labels fail with evidence_label mismatch

bluesky.route_or_idea: Keep evidence labels explicit in every loop.
bluesky.best_case: No one upgrades Partial or Prefilter into win language by accident.
bluesky.smallest_useful_experiment: Reject a loop where Redsky and loop labels disagree.
bluesky.bounded_alternative: Summarize labels in the output line.
bluesky.hidden_assumption: A single label per loop is enough for process packets.
bluesky.stop_condition: Stop if a route needs separate labels for multiple artifacts.

redsky.route_or_claim: Mixed labels can still be safe if the prose explains them.
redsky.current_public_truth_checked: frontier d44cad3/q1152/1571592960; label check is process-only
redsky.strongest_objection: Fleet readers skim labels first and may miss caveats.
redsky.fastest_falsifier: Require exact label agreement inside the packet.

loops.finding: An audit finding was not forced to name the artifact changed or the verification command.
loops.implementation: Added implemented, artifact, implementation, and verification checks.
loops.verification: require-implemented mode fails any loop without implemented=true and a nonblank verification

bluesky.route_or_idea: Tie every audit insight to a concrete change.
bluesky.best_case: Five loops produce five durable changes or explicit parked findings.
bluesky.bounded_alternative: Allow parked findings only when require-implemented is not set.
bluesky.hidden_assumption: The task is implementation-focused rather than exploratory.
bluesky.stop_condition: Stop if a finding cannot be scoped to a public-safe artifact.

redsky.route_or_claim: A finding without verification is still useful progress.
redsky.current_public_truth_checked: frontier d44cad3/q1152/1571592960; no candidate evidence included
redsky.strongest_objection: Unverified process edits can break the harness.
redsky.fastest_falsifier: Run py_compile and the public harness check.

loops.finding: A requested five-loop audit could silently become fewer loops after edits.
loops.implementation: The gate defaults to exact five-loop validation and keeps --exact-loops configurable.
loops.verification: gate output includes loops=5; a one-loop overclaim fixture is checked with --exact-loops 1

bluesky.best_case: The user can ask for five loops and get a verifiable five-loop artifact.
bluesky.smallest_useful_experiment: Fail packets with the wrong loop count.
bluesky.hidden_assumption: Sequential loop numbering is the clearest low-cost check.
bluesky.stop_condition: Stop if loop count enforcement blocks a deliberate exploratory packet.

redsky.route_or_claim: Loop count can be trusted from the final summary alone.
redsky.strongest_objection: Summaries can compress away failed or skipped loops.
redsky.fastest_falsifier: Require sequential loop numbers and the exact count.

loops.finding: Process packets could accidentally carry private paths, alert topics, or tokens into public repo artifacts.
loops.implementation: Added forbidden-text checks for common token prefixes, private home paths, mailbox path, alert topic, and private-key blocks.

bluesky.route_or_idea: Let workers publish public-safe process packets.
bluesky.best_case: The repo can preserve learnings without exposing private coordination surfaces.
bluesky.smallest_useful_experiment: Scan every string in the JSON packet.
bluesky.bounded_alternative: Keep sensitive state in private handoff files and cite only public-safe summaries.
bluesky.hidden_assumption: The forbidden patterns cover the common leak classes.
bluesky.stop_condition: Stop and redact if a packet needs private raw evidence.

redsky.route_or_claim: Audit packets are safe because they are process-level.
redsky.current_public_truth_checked: frontier d44cad3/q1152/1571592960; public-boundary scan is independent of candidate state
redsky.strongest_objection: Process notes often quote exact private paths or alert topics.
redsky.fastest_falsifier: Scan all strings before accepting the packet.
